findings.title: Chargeback freeze is silently reversible by routine Stripe subscription/checkout webhooks (two-writer-one-column clobber)
findings.failure_scenario: LIVE-PROVEN against the deployed API. A tenant is frozen by charge.dispute.created (billing_state='disputed', which is the ONLY thing the tick's freeze guard at tick.ts:63 reads to stop all sends). The customer.subscription.updated handler (billing.ts:160-165) and checkout.session.completed handler (billing.ts:140-149) both write `billing_state` UNCONDITIONALLY — customer.subscription.updated{status:active} overwrites 'disputed' with 'active', and invoice.payment_failed overwrites it with 'past_due'. Stripe emits customer.subscription.updated routinely (renewals, plan changes), so any disputed tenant gets auto-unfrozen by the next ordinary subscription event, resuming sends for exactly the chargeback tenant D5 was built to freeze (SPEC §12: a dispute wave could freeze the master Stripe account). Note the authors KNEW state needed guarding — the dispute.closed(won) path correctly guards `AND billing_state='disputed'` (billing.ts:221) — but the subscription/checkout writers do not. Live proof: dispute.created→'disputed', then subscription.updated{active}→'active'.
findings.fix: Make the freeze sticky. In the customer.subscription.updated, checkout.session.completed, and invoice.payment_failed cases, do not overwrite billing_state when it is currently 'disputed' (mirror the won-path guard, e.g. add `AND billing_state != 'disputed'` to those UPDATEs, or read current state first and skip). Only a won charge.dispute.closed (or an explicit owner action) should clear 'disputed'. Add a test that freezes via dispute.created, delivers a subscription.updated{active}, and asserts billing_state stays 'disputed' and the tick still sends 0.

findings.title: Lifecycle freeze not enforced in the cron deliverability-sweep lane — a frozen tenant can still incur REPLACE_DOMAIN provisioning spend
findings.failure_scenario: runTick returns early for suspended/disputed BEFORE calling runDeliverabilitySweep (tick.ts:57-73), so the tick path is protected. But the standalone TenantDO.deliverabilitySweep() RPC (tenant-do.ts:265) calls runDeliverabilitySweep with NO freeze check, and the cron lane runDeliverabilitySweepAllTenants (admin/ops-sweep.ts:61-75) invokes it for EVERY tenant in the D1 index with no lifecycle filter. Neither a disputed tenant (dispute.created does NOT teardown — domains stay status='active', mailboxes stay live) nor a dunning-suspended tenant (suspendTenant only sets status='suspended', no teardown) is excluded. When such a frozen tenant has a domain whose accumulated complaint/bounce rate crosses the burn threshold, the sweep runs applyReplaceDomain → provisionDomainWithMailboxes (provisioning.ts:30) which BUYS a new domain + provisions mailboxes (+ per-mailbox metering on paid tiers) — real vendor spend at activation for a tenant we deliberately froze to STOP spending/sending. This defeats the 'a frozen tenant does nothing' guarantee, and it is untested (dispute.test.ts only exercises tick(), never deliverabilitySweep()).
findings.fix: Add the same lifecycle freeze guard to runDeliverabilitySweep (deliverability-actions.ts:202) that runTick has: read tenant_profile.status/billing_state and return early with no actions when status='suspended' or billing_state IN ('disputed','canceled','canceling'). Guarding inside runDeliverabilitySweep protects both the cron lane and any future caller. Add a test: freeze a tenant with a pre-injected burning domain, call deliverabilitySweep(), assert zero REPLACE_DOMAIN actions and no new domains/mailboxes.

findings.title: Voluntary cancel doesn't freeze the tick or lock the token — a canceled tenant keeps full write access and can re-provision/relaunch
findings.failure_scenario: LIVE-PROVEN. cancelTenant sets billing_state='canceled'/'canceling' and tears down infra, but (a) the tick freeze guard (tick.ts:63) only checks status='suspended' OR billing_state='disputed' — NOT canceled/canceling; (b) db.ts:40's comment says a canceled tenant keeps its token 'so account() can reflect its canceled state', but the token is FULL-access, not read-only, and cancel deliberately does NOT call setTenantIndexStatus, so resolveTenantFromToken still accepts it; (c) setup-infrastructure (provisioning.ts:113) and launchCampaign have no lifecycle guard. Live proof: after POST /cancel {immediate:true} (account shows billingState 'canceled'), POST /setup-infrastructure returned 202 and POST /campaigns returned 201. Because plan is NOT reset on cancel (stays paid tier), a canceled paid tenant re-provisions fresh healthy mailboxes within its remaining cap, accrues mailbox metering now, and — once the tick cron is armed at activation — sends and incurs real vendor spend, all on an account that 'canceled' and stopped paying. Teardown's reclaim is cosmetic against the tenant's own re-provisioning.
findings.fix: Either extend the tick freeze guard (tick.ts:63) and the provisioning/launch entry points to also reject billing_state IN ('canceled','canceling'), OR — to honor the 'read access after cancel' intent — restrict the canceled tenant's token to read-only routes (gate the write routes on billing_state). Simplest: in runSetupInfrastructure and launchCampaign, throw ValidationError if billing_state is canceled/canceling, and add canceled/canceling to the tick freeze. Add a test that cancels then asserts setup-infrastructure/launch are rejected and a subsequent tick sends 0.

findings.title: Dunning suspension is a one-way trap — a recovered paying tenant can never be un-suspended
findings.failure_scenario: After 4 invoice.payment_failed events, the dunning sweep calls suspendForDunning → suspendTenant sets tenant_profile.status='suspended' (the tick freeze at tick.ts:63). If the customer then fixes payment, Stripe fires customer.subscription.updated{status:active}, which sets billing_state='active' (billing.ts:163) but NOTHING ever resets status back to 'active' — no UPDATE anywhere sets profile.status='active' except the initial INSERT, and there is no reactivation route for the tenant or the owner (routes/admin-ops.ts only has dunning-sweep/digest/terminate). The tenant lands in an inconsistent, permanently-frozen state: account() reports billingState='active' while status='suspended' and every tick sends 0. A transient billing hiccup (expired card later fixed) permanently bricks a now-paying customer.
findings.fix: Add a reactivation path. On customer.subscription.updated/checkout.session.completed mapping to 'active', also clear a dunning suspension: `UPDATE tenant_profile SET status='active' WHERE id=? AND status='suspended'` — but ONLY for dunning suspensions, never for an abuse TERMINATE (distinguish them, e.g. a suspend_reason column, so a terminated tenant is not resurrected). Alternatively add an owner-only POST /admin/tenants/:id/reactivate. Add a test: drive to dunning-suspend, deliver subscription.updated{active}, assert status returns to 'active' and the tick resumes.
